CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-04-2012 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-17-2012 Failure To Post Jury Fees

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-18-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production Of Real Property & For Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
Off Calendar Apr-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jonathan Joiner, Esq., of the Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt, Tel. 415.391.6496, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Michon Spinelli, Esq., of Bledsoe, Cathart, Diestel, Pedersen & Treppa, LLP, Tel. 415.981.5411, appearing for Defendants and opposing party.

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS CARL JONES and RONALD JONES' Motion To Compel Production Of Real Property & For Sanctions is denied. The parties agree that the plaintiff's request for inspection implicates a right to privacy. Plaintiff must meet a higher burden for obtaining discovery that implicates a privacy right. Plaintiff has not met this burden. Plaintiff, who did not file a reply brief, has not made a showing that the request for inspection is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense or that it is essential to a fair resolution of the lawsuit, nor has he established that the information he seeks to discover (which concerns the amount of time the defendants spend in their Fairfield house) cannot be obtained through less intrusive means. See, e.g., Vinson v. Superior Court, 43 Cal.3d 833, 8430844 (1987). Given that the defendants admittedly still occupy the Fairfield house several days per week, the Pro Tem judge is persuaded by the defendants' argument that an inspection is not likely to yield evidence bearing on the question of frequency of use of the Fairfield house, which evidence can be obtained through less intrusive means. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg; Not reported.

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
